description: Hi, you replaced our kitchen panel breaker back in the fall. For the last couple weeks the breaker on the kitchen counter circuit keeps tripping, usually when the microwave and toaster are both on. It held fine all winter so something changed. Can you come take a look?
subject: Breaker tripping on kitchen circuit installed in fall

description: The recessed lights you put in the living room ceiling have started flickering, mostly the two near the window. It is not the dimmer because the others on the same switch are steady. Started about a month ago. Would like it looked at under the warranty.
subject: Recessed light flickering after install

description: The GFCI outlet in the upstairs bathroom will not reset. Push reset and it pops right back out, no power to the outlet or the one downstream. Nothing plugged in that I can see is bad. Can you swing by this week?
subject: GFCI outlet won't reset

description: One of the bedroom outlets is completely dead. The one on the wall by the closet. The rest of the room is fine. We did not change anything. This is the back bedroom you rewired last year.
subject: Dead outlet in back bedroom

description: There is a low humming or buzzing coming from the main panel in the basement. You can hear it when the house is quiet. It is louder than I remember after the upgrade. Want to make sure it is not a problem.
subject: Panel buzzing sound in basement

description: Following up on the 200A service upgrade you finished last fall. The meter side looks fine but the inspector left a sticker about a missing label on the subpanel. Can you come back and finish the labeling so we can close it out?
subject: Follow-up on 200A service upgrade

description: The bathroom exhaust fan you wired up stopped working. The light on the same switch still comes on but the fan does not run at all anymore. No noise from it. Was working fine for months. Callback please.
subject: Bathroom fan wiring callback

description: The double breaker for the dryer and the range trips intermittently. Mostly the dryer side. You set this up during the panel work. It will run for a while then drop out. Happens maybe twice a week now.
subject: Dryer breaker tripping intermittently

description: The under-cabinet lights in the kitchen flicker when the fridge kicks on. They were steady when you installed them. Not a huge deal but figured you would want to know while it is still under warranty.
subject: Under-cabinet lights flicker when fridge cycles

description: Outdoor receptacle on the back patio is dead. GFCI inside the garage reset fine but the patio outlet still has no power. You installed it last summer. We use it for the grill so would like it back working.
subject: Dead outdoor patio outlet

description: The ceiling fan you hung in the master bedroom has started wobbling and there is a faint buzz from the wall switch when it runs on low. Fan itself seems balanced. Could be the controller. Please take a look.
subject: Ceiling fan switch buzzing on low speed

description: Half the outlets in the finished basement went dead at once. Tested the GFCI you put in by the laundry sink and it will not stay reset. Everything downstream is out. This is from the basement circuit you ran.
subject: Basement GFCI won't hold reset, outlets dead

description: The hallway dimmer you installed gets warm to the touch and the LED cans buzz a little at low settings. Lights work fine at full. Wanted to confirm this is normal or if the dimmer needs swapping under warranty.
subject: Dimmer warm and LED cans buzzing

description: Garbage disposal stopped getting power. The switch under the sink does nothing now. Disposal hums if I hit the reset on the unit so I think it is the wiring on the switch you installed during the kitchen job.
subject: No power to garbage disposal switch

description: Following up on the inspection you scheduled after the panel swap. The township wants to do the final walkthrough and asked if you can be present for the AFCI test. Let me know what dates work in the next few weeks.
subject: Follow-up inspection on panel swap

description: The exterior flood light on the motion sensor you wired keeps coming on by itself at night and staying on. Adjusted the sensitivity knob but no change. It is on the circuit you added for the driveway lighting.
subject: Motion flood light staying on at night

description: One leg of the kitchen island circuit is dead. Two of the four outlets on the island have no power. You ran these when we redid the counters. Breaker is not tripped. Would appreciate a callback.
subject: Half of kitchen island outlets dead

description: The new bedroom circuit breaker trips every time the space heater runs for more than ten minutes. It is a 15A and the heater is 1500W. Was fine last month. Not sure if the breaker is weak or it is something else.
subject: Bedroom breaker trips on space heater

description: The smoke and CO combo detector you hardwired in the upstairs hall chirps a low-battery warning even though it is wired in. The others on the same line are quiet. Want it checked before it wakes the whole house again.
subject: Hardwired smoke detector chirping

description: The 240V outlet you added in the garage for the EV charger reads no voltage on one leg. The charger throws a wiring fault. Worked when you commissioned it. Hoping this is a warranty fix.
subject: EV charger outlet reads no voltage on one leg

description: The light over the kitchen sink flickers and sometimes goes off for a second. You replaced that fixture and switch in the fall. Only that one fixture does it. Can you come back and check the connection?
subject: Kitchen sink light flickering after fixture swap

description: The whole-house surge protector you installed at the panel has a red light on the front now. It was green when you put it in. Not sure if that means it took a hit or it failed. Please advise on the warranty.
subject: Surge protector showing red status light

description: The three-way switch setup you wired for the stairwell stopped working from the top of the stairs. The bottom switch still controls the light. Worked correctly after the install. Likely a loose traveler somewhere.
subject: Three-way stairwell switch not working from top

description: The subpanel you added in the garage is warm and one of the breakers has a faint scorched smell. Shut off that breaker for now. This is on the feeder you ran from the main last fall. Please prioritize this one.
subject: Warm subpanel breaker with scorched smell
